questions.stem: Entity A sells merchandise with a list price of $80,000 subject to a trade discount of 15%. The sale is made on credit with terms 2/10, n/30. At what amount should Entity A initially record the trade receivable?
questions.explanation: Trade discounts are never recorded in the accounts; they are used only to determine the invoice price. Invoice price = $80,000 × (1 − 15%) = $68,000. The cash (sales) discount of 2/10 is a separate matter handled at collection (or estimated as variable consideration); the receivable is initially recorded at the gross invoice price of $68,000 under the gross method.
questions.explanationJa: 値引（trade discount）は帳簿に記録せず、請求価格の決定にのみ使用する。請求価格 = $80,000 ×（1 − 15%）= $68,000。現金割引2/10は回収時に処理する別個の事項であり、総額法では債権を請求総額$68,000で当初計上する。

questions.stem: Entity B sells goods for $50,000 on terms 2/10, n/30 and records the receivable using the net method. The customer pays after the discount period has lapsed. What entry does Entity B make to record the forfeited discount upon collection?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Credit Sales Revenue $1,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Credit Sales Discounts Forfeited $1,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Sales Discounts $1,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Credit Accounts Receivable $1,000

questions.explanation: Under the net method, the receivable and revenue are recorded at $50,000 × (1 − 2%) = $49,000, assuming the discount will be taken. When the customer pays after the discount period, the additional $1,000 collected is recognized as 'Sales Discounts Forfeited,' an item of other income. The collection entry is: Debit Cash $50,000, Credit Accounts Receivable $49,000, Credit Sales Discounts Forfeited $1,000.
questions.explanationJa: 純額法では、割引が利用されると仮定し債権と収益を$50,000 ×（1 − 2%）= $49,000で計上する。顧客が割引期間経過後に支払う場合、追加で受け取る$1,000を「失効売上割引（Sales Discounts Forfeited）」として営業外収益に認識する。回収仕訳：（借）現金$50,000 /（貸）売掛金$49,000、（貸）失効売上割引$1,000。

questions.stem: Entity C's allowance for credit losses on trade receivables was $90,000 at the prior year-end. Conditions improved, and at the current year-end the required allowance under CECL is $62,000. There were no write-offs or recoveries during the year. What amount should Entity C report as credit loss expense (or reversal) for the current year?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Credit loss expense of $28,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reversal of credit loss expense of $28,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Credit loss expense of $62,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No effect on the income statement

questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-20-35-1, the allowance is adjusted each period through the income statement so that the balance reflects the current estimate of expected credit losses. With no write-offs or recoveries, the allowance moves from $90,000 to $62,000, a decrease of $28,000. This decrease is recognized as a reversal of credit loss expense (a credit to the income statement) of $28,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-35-1により、引当金は毎期、予想信用損失の現在の見積りを反映するよう損益を通じて調整する。償却・回収がないため、引当金は$90,000から$62,000へ$28,000減少する。この減少は$28,000の信用損失費用の戻入（損益計算書への貸方）として認識する。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Entity D accepts a 4-year, non-interest-bearing note with a face value of $400,000 in exchange for a parcel of land. The market rate for such a note is 9%. The present value of $1 at 9% for 4 periods is 0.70843. At what amount should the note receivable be initially recorded?
questions.explanation: A non-interest-bearing note is recorded at the present value of the single future payment using the market rate. PV = $400,000 × 0.70843 = $283,372. The difference between the $400,000 face value and the $283,372 present value is a $116,628 discount that is amortized to interest income over the 4-year term using the effective interest method.
questions.explanationJa: 無利息手形は市場利率を用いた将来の単一支払額の現在価値で計上する。PV = $400,000 × 0.70843 = $283,372。額面$400,000と現在価値$283,372の差額$116,628は割引であり、実効金利法により4年間で受取利息として償却する。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Entity E receives a 4-year, non-interest-bearing note with a face value of $400,000 when the market rate is 9%. The initial carrying amount is $283,372. What is the interest income Entity E recognizes in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under the effective interest method, interest income for the first year equals the beginning carrying amount times the market rate. Interest income = $283,372 × 9% = $25,503.48, which rounds to $25,504. This amount is added to the carrying value (discount amortization) since the note is non-interest-bearing and no cash interest is received.
questions.explanationJa: 実効金利法では、初年度の受取利息は期首帳簿価額に市場利率を乗じたものに等しい。受取利息 = $283,372 × 9% = $25,503.48 ≈ $25,504。無利息手形で現金利息の受取がないため、この金額は帳簿価額に加算（割引償却）される。

questions.subtopic: Note Receivable - Stated Above Market Premium
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Entity F accepts a 3-year note receivable with a face value of $300,000 and a stated rate of 12% (interest paid annually) when the market rate is 8%. The PV of an ordinary annuity at 8% for 3 periods is 2.57710, and the PV of $1 at 8% for 3 periods is 0.79383. At what amount should the note be initially recorded?
questions.explanation: Because the stated rate (12%) exceeds the market rate (8%), the note is recorded at a premium. Annual interest = $300,000 × 12% = $36,000. PV of interest = $36,000 × 2.57710 = $92,776. PV of principal = $300,000 × 0.79383 = $238,149. Total carrying amount = $92,776 + $238,149 = $330,925. The $30,925 premium is amortized as a reduction of interest income over the note's life.

questions.subtopic: Note Receivable - Premium Amortization Year 1 Income
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Entity G holds a 3-year, $300,000 note receivable with a 12% stated rate (interest paid annually) and a market rate of 8%. The note's initial carrying amount is $330,925. What is the interest income recognized in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under the effective interest method, interest income = carrying amount × market rate = $330,925 × 8% = $26,474. Cash interest received = $300,000 × 12% = $36,000. The excess of cash received over interest income ($36,000 − $26,474 = $9,526) reduces the carrying amount (premium amortization).
questions.explanationJa: 実効金利法では、受取利息 = 帳簿価額 × 市場利率 = $330,925 × 8% = $26,474。受取現金利息 = $300,000 × 12% = $36,000。受取現金が受取利息を超える額（$36,000 − $26,474 = $9,526）は帳簿価額を減額する（プレミアム償却）。

questions.stem: Entity H holds a $60,000, 6-month, 10% interest-bearing note receivable dated March 1. On June 1, Entity H discounts the note at the bank at a 12% discount rate. What proceeds does Entity H receive from the bank?
questions.explanation: Maturity value = $60,000 + ($60,000 × 10% × 6/12) = $60,000 + $3,000 = $63,000. The note is discounted on June 1, which is 3 months after March 1, leaving a remaining discount period of 6 − 3 = 3 months. Bank discount = $63,000 × 12% × 3/12 = $1,890. Proceeds = $63,000 − $1,890 = $61,110.

questions.subtopic: Factoring Without Recourse - Loss on Sale
questions.stem: Entity J factors $750,000 of accounts receivable without recourse. The factor charges a 4% finance fee and withholds 6% as a holdback to cover sales returns and allowances. What is the loss on sale of receivables recognized by Entity J?
questions.explanation: In a without-recourse factoring that qualifies as a sale, the finance fee is the loss on sale. Finance fee = $750,000 × 4% = $30,000. The holdback of $750,000 × 6% = $45,000 is recorded as a receivable from the factor (returned after collections), not a loss. Cash received = $750,000 − $30,000 − $45,000 = $675,000. The loss on sale is the $30,000 finance fee.
questions.explanationJa: 売却適格な遡及権なしファクタリングでは、金融手数料が売却損となる。金融手数料 = $750,000 × 4% = $30,000。留保金$750,000 × 6% = $45,000はファクターからの受取債権（回収完了後に返還）であり損失ではない。受取現金 = $750,000 − $30,000 − $45,000 = $675,000。売却損は金融手数料$30,000である。

questions.stem: Entity K factors $1,000,000 of accounts receivable with recourse. The factor charges a 5% finance fee and withholds 8% as a holdback (recorded as a due-from-factor receivable). Entity K estimates the fair value of the recourse obligation at $35,000. Assuming the transfer qualifies as a sale, what are the net proceeds under ASC 860 (the fair value of all assets obtained less any liabilities incurred)?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 860-20-30-1, net proceeds equal the fair value of all assets obtained less any liabilities incurred. Cash received = $1,000,000 − 5% fee ($50,000) − 8% holdback ($80,000) = $870,000. The 8% holdback of $80,000 is an asset obtained (due from factor). The recourse obligation of $35,000 is a liability incurred. Net proceeds = Cash $870,000 + Due from factor $80,000 − Recourse obligation $35,000 = $915,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 860-20-30-1により、純受取額は取得した全資産の公正価値から発生した負債を控除した額に等しい。受取現金 = $1,000,000 − 手数料5%（$50,000）− 留保金8%（$80,000）= $870,000。留保金8%の$80,000は取得資産（ファクターからの受取債権）。遡及義務$35,000は発生負債。純受取額 = 現金$870,000 + 受取債権$80,000 − 遡及義務$35,000 = $915,000。

questions.subtopic: Pledging - Interest Expense on Secured Borrowing
questions.stem: On October 1, Entity L pledges $500,000 of accounts receivable as collateral for a $350,000 borrowing bearing 10% annual interest. The receivables remain on Entity L's books. What amount of interest expense related to this borrowing should Entity L accrue at December 31?
questions.explanation: Pledging does not derecognize the receivables; the borrowing is a liability and interest accrues on the loan principal. Interest accrues for 3 months (October 1 to December 31). Interest expense = $350,000 × 10% × 3/12 = $8,750. The interest is computed on the $350,000 loan, not on the $500,000 of pledged receivables.
questions.explanationJa: 担保差入では債権の認識中止は行わず、借入金は負債としてローン元本に利息が発生する。利息は3か月分（10月1日から12月31日）発生する。支払利息 = $350,000 × 10% × 3/12 = $8,750。利息は担保差入債権$500,000ではなく借入金$350,000に対して計算する。

questions.subtopic: CECL - Loss Rate Method on Pool
questions.stem: Entity M holds a homogeneous pool of trade receivables totaling $2,500,000. Its historical annual loss rate is 1.8%. Management determines that forward-looking macroeconomic conditions warrant a downward qualitative adjustment of 0.3 percentage points. What is the required allowance for credit losses?
questions.explanation: Under the CECL loss-rate method, the historical loss rate is adjusted for current conditions and reasonable and supportable forecasts. Adjusted loss rate = 1.8% − 0.3% = 1.5%. Required allowance = $2,500,000 × 1.5% = $37,500. The favorable forecast reduces the loss rate, lowering the allowance compared with the unadjusted $45,000.
questions.explanationJa: CECL損失率法では、過去の損失率を現在の状況および合理的かつ裏付け可能な予測について調整する。調整後損失率 = 1.8% − 0.3% = 1.5%。必要引当金 = $2,500,000 × 1.5% = $37,500。良好な予測により損失率が低下し、未調整の$45,000より引当金が減少する。

questions.stem: Entity N estimates expected credit losses on a $1,000,000 receivable using a probability-of-default approach. It assigns a 6% probability of default (PD) and a 40% loss given default (LGD). What is the expected credit loss?
questions.explanation: The probability-of-default method computes expected credit loss as the exposure at default multiplied by the probability of default and the loss given default. ECL = $1,000,000 × 6% × 40% = $24,000. The PD reflects the likelihood the borrower defaults, and the LGD reflects the portion not expected to be recovered upon default.
questions.explanationJa: デフォルト確率法では、予想信用損失をデフォルト時エクスポージャー × デフォルト確率 × デフォルト時損失率で計算する。ECL = $1,000,000 × 6% × 40% = $24,000。PDは借手がデフォルトする可能性、LGDはデフォルト時に回収が見込めない割合を反映する。

questions.subtopic: Note Receivable - Effective Interest Final Year Carrying
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Entity O accepts a 2-year, $200,000 note receivable with a stated rate of 5% (interest paid annually) when the market rate is 9%. The initial carrying amount is $185,927. Interest income in Year 1 is $16,733. What is the carrying amount of the note at December 31, Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under the effective interest method, the carrying amount increases by the discount amortized. Cash interest received in Year 1 = $200,000 × 5% = $10,000. Discount amortization = Interest income $16,733 − Cash interest $10,000 = $6,733. Carrying amount at year-end = $185,927 + $6,733 = $192,660.
questions.explanationJa: 実効金利法では、帳簿価額は償却された割引額だけ増加する。Year 1の受取現金利息 = $200,000 × 5% = $10,000。割引償却額 = 受取利息$16,733 − 現金利息$10,000 = $6,733。期末帳簿価額 = $185,927 + $6,733 = $192,660。

questions.subtopic: Note Exchanged for Cash and Rights
questions.stem: Entity P lends $90,000 cash to a supplier and receives a 3-year, 3% note with a face value of $90,000 (interest paid annually). In exchange for the below-market rate, the supplier grants Entity P a purchase price reduction on future inventory. The market rate is 8%. The PV of the note's cash flows at 8% is $78,401. How should Entity P record the $11,599 difference at inception?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As interest income immediately

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a discount on note receivable and a prepaid asset (deferred charge) for the future price reduction

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a loss on the note

choices.label: D
choices.text: As an increase to the note receivable carrying amount

questions.explanation: Under ASC 835-30-25-3, when a note is issued for cash and an additional right or privilege, the difference between the cash advanced ($90,000) and the present value of the note ($78,401) represents payment for that right. The $11,599 difference is recorded as a discount on the note receivable, with a corresponding prepaid asset (deferred charge) for the future inventory price reduction. The discount is amortized to interest income, and the prepaid asset reduces future inventory cost.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-30-25-3により、現金と追加の権利・特典のために手形が発行された場合、貸付現金（$90,000）と手形の現在価値（$78,401）の差額はその権利の対価を表す。差額$11,599は受取手形の割引として記録し、将来の在庫価格引下げに対する前払資産（繰延費用）を計上する。割引は受取利息に償却し、前払資産は将来の在庫原価を減額する。

questions.subtopic: Bad Debt Expense - Aging Method with Debit Balance
questions.stem: At year-end, Entity Q's aging analysis indicates a required allowance for credit losses of $52,000. Before adjustment, the allowance account has a debit balance of $7,000 (write-offs exceeded the prior allowance). What amount of credit loss expense should Entity Q record?
questions.explanation: Under the aging (percentage-of-receivables) method, the expense is the amount needed to bring the allowance to the required ending credit balance. Because the allowance currently has a $7,000 debit balance, the expense must cover both the debit balance and the required $52,000 credit balance. Credit loss expense = $52,000 + $7,000 = $59,000.
questions.explanationJa: 年齢分析（売掛金残高比率）法では、費用は引当金を必要な期末貸方残高にするために要する金額である。引当金が現在$7,000の借方残高であるため、費用はこの借方残高と必要な貸方残高$52,000の両方をカバーしなければならない。信用損失費用 = $52,000 + $7,000 = $59,000。

questions.stem: Entity R sells property with a cost of $360,000 for $600,000 using the installment method. The buyer pays $150,000 down in Year 1 and $150,000 in Year 2. What gross profit does Entity R recognize in Year 2?
questions.explanation: Gross profit rate = ($600,000 − $360,000) / $600,000 = 40%. Under the installment method, gross profit is recognized as cash is collected. Year 2 cash collection = $150,000. Year 2 gross profit = $150,000 × 40% = $60,000. The down payment recognition occurred separately in Year 1.
questions.explanationJa: 粗利益率 = （$600,000 − $360,000）/ $600,000 = 40%。割賦販売法では、粗利益は現金回収に応じて認識する。Year 2の現金回収 = $150,000。Year 2粗利益 = $150,000 × 40% = $60,000。頭金の認識はYear 1に別途行われた。

questions.stem: Entity S sells land costing $400,000 for $700,000 and, because collectibility is highly uncertain, uses the cost recovery method. The buyer pays $250,000 in Year 1 and $250,000 in Year 2. How much gross profit does Entity S recognize in Year 2?
questions.explanation: Under the cost recovery method, no gross profit is recognized until cumulative cash collections exceed the cost of the item sold ($400,000). Year 1 collections of $250,000 do not exceed cost, so no profit is recognized. By the end of Year 2, cumulative collections = $250,000 + $250,000 = $500,000, which exceeds the $400,000 cost by $100,000. Therefore $100,000 of gross profit is recognized in Year 2.
questions.explanationJa: 原価回収法では、累積現金回収額が販売物の原価（$400,000）を超えるまで粗利益を認識しない。Year 1の回収$250,000は原価を超えないため利益を認識しない。Year 2末の累積回収額 = $250,000 + $250,000 = $500,000で、原価$400,000を$100,000超過する。したがってYear 2に粗利益$100,000を認識する。

questions.subtopic: Note Impairment - PV of Restructured Cash Flows
questions.stem: Bank T holds a $600,000 loan with an original effective interest rate of 10%. The borrower is in financial difficulty, and the bank now expects a single lump-sum payment of $500,000 at the end of 2 years (no interim interest). The PV of $1 at 10% for 2 periods is 0.82645. What credit loss should the bank recognize?
questions.explanation: The expected credit loss equals the carrying amount less the present value of expected future cash flows discounted at the original effective rate. PV of expected cash flows = $500,000 × 0.82645 = $413,225. Credit loss = $600,000 − $413,225 = $186,775.
questions.explanationJa: 予想信用損失 = 帳簿価額 − 当初実効金利で割り引いた予想将来キャッシュフローの現在価値。予想キャッシュフローのPV = $500,000 × 0.82645 = $413,225。信用損失 = $600,000 − $413,225 = $186,775。

questions.stem: Bank U holds a $900,000 loan that is collateral-dependent. The fair value of the collateral is $780,000 and estimated costs to sell are $30,000. Repayment is expected substantially through sale of the collateral. Under the CECL collateral-dependent practical expedient, what allowance for credit losses should the bank record?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-20-35-5, for a collateral-dependent financial asset, the allowance equals the amortized cost less the fair value of the collateral, adjusted for costs to sell when repayment is expected through sale. Collateral fair value less costs to sell = $780,000 − $30,000 = $750,000. Allowance = $900,000 − $750,000 = $150,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-35-5により、担保依存型の金融資産では、引当金は償却原価から担保の公正価値（売却が見込まれる場合は売却費用調整後）を控除した額に等しい。担保の公正価値から売却費用を控除した額 = $780,000 − $30,000 = $750,000。引当金 = $900,000 − $750,000 = $150,000。

questions.stem: Entity V purchases a portfolio of loans for $850,000 that meets the definition of purchased financial assets with credit deterioration (PCD). At acquisition, the entity estimates expected credit losses of $120,000. Under ASC 326, what is the initial amortized cost basis (gross) of the loans?
questions.explanation: For purchased credit-deteriorated (PCD) assets, ASC 326-20-30-13 requires a gross-up approach: the initial allowance for expected credit losses is added to the purchase price to determine the initial amortized cost basis. There is no credit loss expense at acquisition. Initial amortized cost = Purchase price $850,000 + Initial allowance $120,000 = $970,000.
questions.explanationJa: 購入時信用劣化（PCD）資産については、ASC 326-20-30-13がグロスアップ法を要求する。予想信用損失の当初引当金を購入価格に加算して当初償却原価を算定する。取得時に信用損失費用は計上しない。当初償却原価 = 購入価格$850,000 + 当初引当金$120,000 = $970,000。

questions.subtopic: Sales Return Allowance - Net Receivable Impact
questions.stem: Entity W has gross accounts receivable of $1,200,000, an allowance for credit losses of $36,000, and a separate refund liability of $48,000 for expected sales returns recorded under ASC 606. At what amount should accounts receivable, net of the credit loss allowance, be presented in current assets?
questions.explanation: The allowance for credit losses is a contra-asset that reduces accounts receivable. The refund liability for expected returns is a separate liability under ASC 606-10-32-10, not a contra-receivable, so it is presented on the liability side and does not reduce the receivable. Accounts receivable, net = $1,200,000 − $36,000 = $1,164,000.
questions.explanationJa: 貸倒引当金は売掛金を減額するcontra-assetである。返品見込みに対する返金負債はASC 606-10-32-10に基づく別個の負債であり、債権の控除項目ではないため、負債側に表示され債権を減額しない。売掛金純額 = $1,200,000 − $36,000 = $1,164,000。

questions.subtopic: Accounts Receivable Turnover - Days Sales Outstanding
questions.stem: Entity X reports net credit sales of $7,300,000 for the year. Beginning accounts receivable were $550,000 and ending accounts receivable were $810,000. What is the days sales outstanding (DSO), using a 365-day year and average receivables?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 34.0 days

choices.label: B
choices.text: 68.0 days

choices.label: C
choices.text: 40.5 days

choices.label: D
choices.text: 27.5 days

questions.explanation: Average accounts receivable = ($550,000 + $810,000) / 2 = $680,000. Receivables turnover = Net credit sales / Average AR = $7,300,000 / $680,000 = 10.735 times. Days sales outstanding = 365 / 10.735 = 34.0 days. Equivalently, DSO = (Average AR / Net credit sales) × 365 = ($680,000 / $7,300,000) × 365 = 34.0 days.

questions.stem: On May 1, Entity Y accepts an $180,000, 1-year note receivable bearing 8% interest, with all interest due at maturity. What amount of interest receivable should Entity Y report on its December 31 year-end balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Interest accrues from May 1 to December 31, which is 8 months. Annual interest = $180,000 × 8% = $14,400. Interest receivable accrued = $14,400 × 8/12 = $9,600. Although the cash interest is not received until maturity, the earned interest must be accrued at year-end.
questions.explanationJa: 利息は5月1日から12月31日までの8か月分発生する。年間利息 = $180,000 × 8% = $14,400。発生未収利息 = $14,400 × 8/12 = $9,600。現金利息は満期まで受け取らないが、稼得した利息は期末に見越計上する。

questions.stem: Entity Z, a U.S. company, sells goods to a U.K. customer for 500,000 British pounds on December 1, when the spot rate is $1.26/pound. At December 31, the spot rate is $1.31/pound. The receivable is still outstanding at year-end. What foreign currency transaction gain or loss should Entity Z recognize at December 31?
questions.explanation: The receivable is denominated in pounds and must be remeasured at the year-end spot rate. Initial value = 500,000 × $1.26 = $630,000. December 31 value = 500,000 × $1.31 = $655,000. Because the pound strengthened, the dollar value of the receivable increased by $655,000 − $630,000 = $25,000, producing a foreign currency transaction gain of $25,000 (ASC 830-20-35-1).
questions.explanationJa: 債権はポンド建てであり、期末スポットレートで再測定しなければならない。当初価額 = 500,000 × $1.26 = $630,000。12月31日価額 = 500,000 × $1.31 = $655,000。ポンド高により債権のドル価値が$655,000 − $630,000 = $25,000増加し、為替差益$25,000を生じる（ASC 830-20-35-1）。

questions.stem: Under ASC 606, which of the following best distinguishes a contract asset from a receivable?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A contract asset and a receivable are identical and used interchangeably

choices.label: B
choices.text: A receivable is an unconditional right to consideration, whereas a contract asset is a right to consideration conditioned on something other than the passage of time

choices.label: C
choices.text: A contract asset is always classified as noncurrent, while a receivable is always current

choices.label: D
choices.text: A receivable arises only from service contracts, while a contract asset arises only from goods

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-45-3 and 45-4, a receivable is an entity's unconditional right to consideration — only the passage of time is required before payment is due. A contract asset is the entity's right to consideration in exchange for goods or services already transferred when that right is conditioned on something other than the passage of time (for example, completing additional performance obligations).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-45-3および45-4により、債権（receivable）は対価に対する無条件の権利であり、支払期日までに時の経過のみを要する。契約資産（contract asset）は、既に移転した財・サービスと交換に対価を受け取る権利のうち、時の経過以外の条件（例：追加の履行義務の完了）が付されたものである。

questions.subtopic: Note Receivable - Land Sale Gain with PV
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Entity AA sells land with a carrying value of $250,000 in exchange for a 3-year, non-interest-bearing note with a face value of $360,000. No reliable fair value of the land is available, but the market rate for such a note is 10%. The PV of $1 at 10% for 3 periods is 0.75131. What gain on sale of land should Entity AA recognize at the date of sale?
questions.explanation: When the fair value of the property is not determinable, the note is recorded at the present value of its cash flows using the imputed market rate. PV of note = $360,000 × 0.75131 = $270,472. This present value is the deemed sales price. Gain on sale = $270,472 − Carrying value $250,000 = $20,472. The $89,528 discount ($360,000 − $270,472) is recognized as interest income over the note term, not as gain at sale.
questions.explanationJa: 資産の公正価値が判定できない場合、手形は帰属市場利率を用いたキャッシュフローの現在価値で計上する。手形のPV = $360,000 × 0.75131 = $270,472。この現在価値がみなし販売価格となる。売却益 = $270,472 − 帳簿価額$250,000 = $20,472。割引$89,528（$360,000 − $270,472）は売却時の利得ではなく、手形期間にわたり受取利息として認識する。

questions.stem: Entity BB has an unfunded loan commitment of $4,000,000 that is not unconditionally cancellable by the entity. Under CECL, it estimates that 60% of the commitment is likely to be funded over its life and applies an expected lifetime loss rate of 2% to the funded exposure. What liability for expected credit losses should Entity BB record on the unfunded commitment?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-20-30-11, expected credit losses on off-balance-sheet credit exposures that are not unconditionally cancellable are estimated over the contractual period, considering the likelihood that funding will occur. Expected funded exposure = $4,000,000 × 60% = $2,400,000. Expected credit loss liability = $2,400,000 × 2% = $48,000, recorded as a liability rather than a contra-asset.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-30-11により、無条件に取消不能でないオフバランスの信用エクスポージャーの予想信用損失は、資金供与が生じる可能性を考慮し契約期間にわたり見積もる。予想資金供与エクスポージャー = $4,000,000 × 60% = $2,400,000。予想信用損失負債 = $2,400,000 × 2% = $48,000で、contra-assetではなく負債として計上する。

questions.stem: Before a write-off, Entity CC has gross accounts receivable of $940,000 and an allowance for credit losses of $58,000, giving net receivables of $882,000. Entity CC then writes off a specific $22,000 account as uncollectible using the allowance method. What is the net realizable value of accounts receivable immediately after the write-off?
questions.explanation: A write-off under the allowance method reduces both gross accounts receivable and the allowance by the same amount: gross AR becomes $940,000 − $22,000 = $918,000, and the allowance becomes $58,000 − $22,000 = $36,000. Net realizable value = $918,000 − $36,000 = $882,000, unchanged from before the write-off. The write-off has no effect on net receivables or net income because the loss was anticipated in the allowance.
questions.explanationJa: 引当金法による償却は、売掛金総額と引当金を同額減少させる。売掛金総額は$940,000 − $22,000 = $918,000、引当金は$58,000 − $22,000 = $36,000となる。正味実現可能価額 = $918,000 − $36,000 = $882,000で、償却前と変わらない。損失は引当金で既に予想されていたため、償却は正味債権にも純利益にも影響しない。

questions.stem: Entity A sells merchandise for $90,000 on terms 1/10, n/30 and records the sale using the gross method. The customer pays $60,000 of the invoice within the discount period and the remaining $30,000 after the discount period expires. What amount should Entity A report as sales discounts for this transaction?
questions.explanation: Under the gross method, the receivable is recorded at the full invoice price of $90,000, and sales discounts are recorded only when customers actually pay within the discount period. Only $60,000 of the invoice was paid within the discount period, so the discount is $60,000 × 1% = $600. The amount of $900 would result from incorrectly applying the 1% discount to the entire $90,000 invoice, $300 would result from applying the discount to the $30,000 portion that was paid late and earned no discount, and $0 would be correct only if no payment had been received within the discount period.
questions.explanationJa: 総額法（gross method）では売掛金を請求額$90,000の総額で計上し、売上割引（sales discounts）は顧客が実際に割引期間内に支払った時点でのみ認識します。割引期間内に支払われたのは$60,000のみなので、割引額は $60,000 × 1% = $600 です。$900は請求額全額$90,000に1%を適用した誤り、$300は割引が適用されない遅延支払分$30,000に適用した誤りです。ASC 606上、売上割引は変動対価として取引価格から控除されます。

questions.stem: Entity B sells goods for $200,000 on terms 2/10, n/30 and records the receivable using the net method. The customer pays the full invoice price after the discount period has expired. How should Entity B account for the $4,000 difference between the cash received and the recorded receivable?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As an addition to sales revenue in the period of sale

choices.label: B
choices.text: As sales discounts forfeited, reported as other income

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a reduction of the allowance for credit losses

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a deferred credit amortized over the collection period

questions.explanation: Under the net method, the receivable is initially recorded at $200,000 × 98% = $196,000, on the assumption that the customer will take the discount. When the customer pays the gross amount of $200,000 after the discount period, the $4,000 excess represents sales discounts forfeited, which is reported as other income (interest-like income for financing the customer). Adding the amount to sales revenue is inappropriate because the sale was already measured at the cash-equivalent price; the allowance for credit losses is unrelated to discount forfeitures; and there is no basis for deferring the amount, since it is earned when the discount period lapses.
questions.explanationJa: 純額法（net method)では、顧客が割引を利用すると仮定して売掛金を $200,000 × 98% = $196,000 で当初計上します。顧客が割引期間経過後に総額$200,000を支払った場合、差額$4,000は「失効売上割引（sales discounts forfeited）」として営業外収益（その他の収益）に計上します。売上高への加算は、売上が既に現金等価額で測定されているため不適切です。貸倒引当金とは無関係であり、割引期間の経過時点で稼得されるため繰延処理の根拠もありません。

questions.stem: Entity C sells merchandise with a list price of $100,000, subject to chain trade discounts of 20% and 10%. What amount should Entity C record as the accounts receivable and sales revenue at the date of sale?
questions.explanation: Chain (successive) trade discounts are applied sequentially, not added together: $100,000 × (1 − 0.20) × (1 − 0.10) = $100,000 × 0.80 × 0.90 = $72,000. Trade discounts are never recorded in the accounts; the sale and receivable are recorded net of all trade discounts. The amount of $70,000 results from incorrectly adding the discounts (30%) and applying them once, $80,000 applies only the 20% discount, and $90,000 applies only the 10% discount.
questions.explanationJa: 連鎖（逐次）トレードディスカウントは合算ではなく順次適用します: $100,000 × (1 − 0.20) × (1 − 0.10) = $72,000。トレードディスカウントは帳簿に記録されず、売上と売掛金はすべてのトレードディスカウント控除後の純額で計上します。$70,000は割引率を単純合算（30%）して一度に適用した誤り、$80,000は20%のみ、$90,000は10%のみを適用した誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Percentage of Receivables - Existing Credit Balance
questions.stem: At year-end, Entity D has accounts receivable of $750,000 and estimates that 4% of the outstanding balance will be uncollectible. Before adjustment, the allowance for credit losses has a credit balance of $8,000. What amount should Entity D record as credit loss expense for the year?
questions.explanation: Under the percentage-of-receivables approach, the calculation determines the required ending balance of the allowance: $750,000 × 4% = $30,000. Because the allowance already has a credit balance of $8,000, the expense needed to bring the allowance to the required level is $30,000 − $8,000 = $22,000. The amount of $30,000 ignores the existing credit balance, $38,000 incorrectly adds the existing balance to the required balance, and $8,000 is merely the unadjusted balance.
questions.explanationJa: 売掛金残高比率法では、計算結果は引当金の「必要期末残高」を示します: $750,000 × 4% = $30,000。調整前に貸方残高$8,000が既にあるため、必要な繰入額（費用）は $30,000 − $8,000 = $22,000 です。$30,000は既存残高を無視した誤り、$38,000は既存残高を必要残高に加算した誤り、$8,000は単なる調整前残高です。ASC 326の下でも、引当金を必要水準に調整するという仕組みは同様です。

questions.subtopic: Percentage of Sales - Ending Allowance Balance
questions.stem: Entity E estimates credit loss expense at 1.5% of credit sales. Credit sales for the year were $4,000,000. The allowance for credit losses had a beginning credit balance of $25,000, and accounts totaling $40,000 were written off during the year. There were no recoveries. What is the ending balance of the allowance for credit losses?
questions.explanation: Under the percentage-of-sales approach, the expense is computed directly: $4,000,000 × 1.5% = $60,000, regardless of the existing allowance balance. The ending allowance is then: beginning balance $25,000 + expense $60,000 − write-offs $40,000 = $45,000. The amount of $60,000 is the expense itself, not the ending allowance; $20,000 omits the beginning balance ($60,000 − $40,000); and $65,000 incorrectly adds write-offs instead of subtracting them ($25,000 + $40,000).
questions.explanationJa: 売上高比率法では費用を直接計算します: $4,000,000 × 1.5% = $60,000（既存の引当金残高にかかわらず）。期末引当金残高は、期首残高$25,000 ＋ 繰入$60,000 − 償却$40,000 = $45,000 です。$60,000は費用そのものであり期末残高ではありません。$20,000は期首残高を omit した誤り、$65,000は償却を減算ではなく加算した誤りです。

questions.stem: Entity F's allowance for credit losses had a beginning balance of $62,000 and an ending balance of $51,000. During the year, credit loss expense of $48,000 was recorded and previously written-off accounts of $5,000 were recovered and collected. What amount of receivables was written off during the year?
questions.explanation: The allowance rollforward is: beginning balance + credit loss expense + recoveries − write-offs = ending balance. Recoveries are credited back to the allowance when the account is reinstated. Solving: $62,000 + $48,000 + $5,000 − write-offs = $51,000, so write-offs = $64,000. The amount of $54,000 results from subtracting rather than adding recoveries, $59,000 ignores recoveries entirely, and $70,000 has no basis in the rollforward relationship.
questions.explanationJa: 引当金のロールフォワードは「期首残高 ＋ 繰入額 ＋ 回収額（戻入） − 償却額 ＝ 期末残高」です。償却済債権の回収時は、まず債権を復活させ引当金を貸方記入します。$62,000 + $48,000 + $5,000 − 償却額 = $51,000 より、償却額 = $64,000。$54,000は回収額を加算ではなく減算した誤り、$59,000は回収額を無視した誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Aging Method - Expense with Existing Credit Balance
questions.stem: Entity G prepares the following aging of its $820,000 of accounts receivable at year-end: current $500,000 (1% estimated loss), 31–60 days $200,000 (5%), 61–90 days $80,000 (10%), over 90 days $40,000 (40%). Before adjustment, the allowance for credit losses has a credit balance of $12,000. What is credit loss expense for the year?
questions.explanation: The aging schedule determines the required ending allowance: ($500,000 × 1%) + ($200,000 × 5%) + ($80,000 × 10%) + ($40,000 × 40%) = $5,000 + $10,000 + $8,000 + $16,000 = $39,000. Because the allowance already has a $12,000 credit balance, the expense is $39,000 − $12,000 = $27,000. The amount of $39,000 is the required ending balance, not the expense; $51,000 incorrectly adds the existing balance; and $16,000 is only the loss estimate on the over-90-day category.
questions.explanationJa: エイジング法では必要な期末引当金残高を算定します: ($500,000×1%) + ($200,000×5%) + ($80,000×10%) + ($40,000×40%) = $39,000。調整前貸方残高$12,000があるため、繰入額（費用）は $39,000 − $12,000 = $27,000 です。$39,000は必要期末残高であって費用ではなく、$51,000は既存残高を加算した誤り、$16,000は90日超カテゴリーの見積額のみです。

questions.subtopic: Direct Write-Off Method - Recovery in Subsequent Year
questions.stem: Entity H uses the direct write-off method (immaterial amounts) for uncollectible accounts. In Year 1, it wrote off a $9,000 receivable directly to bad debt expense. In Year 2, the customer unexpectedly paid the $9,000 in full. How should Entity H record the Year 2 collection?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Credit the allowance for credit losses for $9,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Credit accounts receivable for $9,000 with no income effect

choices.label: C
choices.text: Restate Year 1 financial statements to remove the write-off

choices.label: D
choices.text: Credit a recovery of bad debts (income) account for $9,000

questions.explanation: Under the direct write-off method, a recovery in a year subsequent to the write-off is recorded by debiting cash and crediting an income account such as uncollectible accounts recovered (or a credit to bad debt expense only if the recovery occurs in the same year as the write-off). There is no allowance account under the direct write-off method, so crediting the allowance is impossible. The receivable was already removed in Year 1, so crediting accounts receivable without an income effect would misstate income. Restatement is inappropriate because the original write-off reflected the information available at the time and is not an error.
questions.explanationJa: 直接償却法の下で、償却した年度の翌年度以降に回収があった場合は、現金を借方、「償却済債権取立益（recovery of bad debts）」等の収益勘定を貸方に記入します（同一年度内の回収なら貸倒損失の戻し入れも可）。直接償却法には引当金勘定が存在しないため、引当金への貸方記入はできません。債権はYear 1に既に消去済みであり、収益認識なしの売掛金貸方記入は損益を歪めます。当初の償却は当時の情報に基づく見積りで誤謬ではないため、遡及修正再表示も不適切です。

questions.stem: Entity J holds an $80,000, 9%, 6-month note receivable from a customer. At maturity, the customer dishonors the note. Entity J believes eventual collection is probable. What amount should Entity J transfer to accounts receivable at the date of dishonor?
questions.explanation: When a note is dishonored but collection is still expected, the full maturity value — face plus accrued interest — is transferred from notes receivable to accounts receivable: $80,000 + ($80,000 × 9% × 6/12) = $80,000 + $3,600 = $83,600. The interest was earned over the note term and remains a valid claim against the maker. The amount of $80,000 omits the earned interest, $87,200 incorrectly uses a full year of interest, and $3,600 is only the interest component.
questions.explanationJa: 手形が不渡り（dishonored）になっても回収が見込まれる場合、満期価値（額面＋経過利息）全額を受取手形から売掛金へ振り替えます: $80,000 + ($80,000 × 9% × 6/12) = $83,600。利息は手形期間にわたり稼得済みで、振出人への正当な請求権です。$80,000は稼得済利息を omit した誤り、$87,200は1年分の利息を使った誤り、$3,600は利息部分のみです。

questions.subtopic: Note Receivable - Stated Rate Equals Market Rate
questions.stem: On January 1, Entity K accepts a 3-year, $150,000 note receivable from a customer in exchange for merchandise. The note bears interest at 7%, payable annually, and the prevailing market rate for notes of similar risk is also 7%. At what amount should the note be recorded at inception?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $150,000 face value

choices.label: B
choices.text: Face value less a discount for imputed interest

choices.label: C
choices.text: Face value plus the present value of three years of interest payments

choices.label: D
choices.text: The undiscounted sum of all principal and interest cash flows

questions.explanation: When the stated rate equals the market rate, the present value of the note's cash flows (principal plus annual interest discounted at 7%) exactly equals the face value, so the note is recorded at $150,000 with no discount or premium. A discount arises only when the stated rate is below the market rate, and recording face value plus the present value of interest would double count the interest cash flows. The undiscounted sum of all cash flows ($150,000 plus $31,500 of interest) overstates the receivable because it ignores the time value of money.
questions.explanationJa: 表面利率（7%）が市場利率（7%）と等しい場合、手形のキャッシュフロー（元本＋年次利息）を市場利率で割り引いた現在価値は額面と一致するため、割引額も打歩もなく額面$150,000で計上します（ASC 835-30）。割引額（discount）が生じるのは表面利率が市場利率を下回る場合のみです。額面に利息の現在価値を加える処理は利息キャッシュフローの二重計上であり、元利合計の未割引額（$181,500）は貨幣の時間価値を無視するため過大計上となります。

questions.stem: On January 1, Entity L accepts a 2-year, non-interest-bearing note with a face value of $50,000 in exchange for consulting services. The market rate of interest for similar notes is 10%, and the present value factor of $1 for 2 periods at 10% is 0.826. What is the balance of the discount on notes receivable at inception?
questions.explanation: The note is recorded at its present value of $50,000 × 0.826 = $41,300, and the discount on notes receivable is the difference between face value and present value: $50,000 − $41,300 = $8,700. The discount is amortized to interest income over the note term using the effective interest method. The amount of $41,300 is the carrying amount of the note, not the discount; $10,000 results from applying simple interest of 10% × 2 years to the face value; and $5,000 represents only one year of simple interest.
questions.explanationJa: 手形は現在価値 $50,000 × 0.826 = $41,300 で計上され、受取手形割引額（discount on notes receivable）は額面と現在価値の差額 $50,000 − $41,300 = $8,700 です。割引額は実効金利法により手形期間にわたって受取利息へ償却されます（ASC 835-30）。$41,300は手形の帳簿価額であって割引額ではなく、$10,000は額面に単利10%×2年を適用した誤り、$5,000は単利1年分のみです。

questions.stem: Entity M discounts a customer's note receivable at a bank on a without-recourse basis. The bank assumes the risk of nonpayment and Entity M surrenders control over the note. How should Entity M account for this transaction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a sale: derecognize the note and recognize any difference between proceeds and carrying amount as a gain or loss

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a secured borrowing: keep the note on the books and record a liability for the proceeds

choices.label: C
choices.text: Keep the note on the books and disclose a contingent liability for the maturity value

choices.label: D
choices.text: Defer any gain or loss until the maker pays the bank at maturity

questions.explanation: Discounting a note without recourse transfers the risk of nonpayment to the bank, and because control has been surrendered, the transfer qualifies as a sale under ASC 860. The note is removed from the books and the difference between the proceeds and the carrying amount (face plus accrued interest earned to date) is recognized immediately as a gain or loss. Secured borrowing treatment applies only when the sale criteria are not met. A contingent liability arises in with-recourse discounting, not without-recourse. Deferring the gain or loss is inconsistent with derecognition at the transfer date.
questions.explanationJa: ノンリコース（without recourse）での手形割引は不払リスクが銀行に移転し、支配も放棄されているため、ASC 860上「売却」として処理します。手形を帳簿から消去し、手取金と帳簿価額（額面＋経過利息）との差額を直ちに売却損益として認識します。担保付借入処理は売却要件を満たさない場合のみ適用されます。偶発債務はリコース付き割引で生じるものであり、損益の繰延は譲渡日での認識中止と矛盾します。

questions.stem: Entity N holds a $120,000, 8%, 9-month note receivable dated January 1. On April 1, Entity N discounts the note at a bank at a discount rate of 12%. What proceeds does Entity N receive from the bank?
questions.explanation: Step 1: Maturity value = $120,000 + ($120,000 × 8% × 9/12) = $120,000 + $7,200 = $127,200. Step 2: The note matures October 1, so 6 months remain when discounted on April 1. Bank discount = $127,200 × 12% × 6/12 = $7,632. Step 3: Proceeds = $127,200 − $7,632 = $119,568. The amount of $120,000 ignores both interest and the discount mechanics, $115,752 incorrectly computes the bank discount over the full 9-month term, and $127,200 is the maturity value before deducting the bank's discount.
questions.explanationJa: 手形割引の手取金は3段階で計算します。①満期価値 = $120,000 + ($120,000 × 8% × 9/12) = $127,200。②満期は10月1日のため、4月1日の割引時点で残存期間は6か月。割引料 = $127,200 × 12% × 6/12 = $7,632。③手取金 = $127,200 − $7,632 = $119,568。割引料は「満期価値」に「銀行の割引率」を「残存期間」で適用する点が重要です。$115,752は割引料を9か月全期間で計算した誤り、$127,200は割引料控除前の満期価値です。

questions.stem: Entity P holds a $40,000, 6%, 6-month note receivable dated July 1. On October 1, Entity P discounts the note without recourse at a bank at a 10% discount rate. What loss should Entity P recognize on the discounting?
questions.explanation: Maturity value = $40,000 + ($40,000 × 6% × 6/12) = $41,200. Three months remain at the October 1 discount date, so the bank discount = $41,200 × 10% × 3/12 = $1,030, and proceeds = $41,200 − $1,030 = $40,170. The carrying amount of the note is face plus 3 months of accrued interest: $40,000 + ($40,000 × 6% × 3/12) = $40,600. Loss = $40,600 − $40,170 = $430. The amount of $1,030 is the bank discount itself, $600 is the accrued interest earned before discounting, and $170 results from incorrectly comparing the proceeds with the face value alone instead of the carrying amount.

questions.stem: Which of the following statements best describes the assignment of accounts receivable under a non-notification arrangement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The assigned receivables are removed from the assignor's balance sheet and a gain or loss is recognized

choices.label: B
choices.text: Customers are instructed to remit payments directly to the finance company

choices.label: C
choices.text: Specific receivables serve as collateral for a loan, remain on the assignor's books, and the assignor typically continues to collect from customers

choices.label: D
choices.text: The finance company assumes the credit risk of the assigned receivables

questions.explanation: An assignment is a secured borrowing in which specifically identified receivables collateralize a loan. The receivables remain on the assignor's balance sheet, a note payable is recorded for the loan, and under a non-notification arrangement the assignor continues to collect from customers and remits collections to the lender. Removal of receivables with gain or loss recognition describes a sale (factoring), direct customer remittance to the finance company describes a notification arrangement, and assumption of credit risk by the transferee describes factoring without recourse.
questions.explanationJa: 売掛金の譲渡担保（assignment）は、特定された売掛金を担保とする担保付借入です。売掛金は譲渡人の貸借対照表に残り、借入金（note payable）を計上します。非通知方式（non-notification basis）では譲渡人が引き続き顧客から回収し、回収額を貸し手に送金します。債権の消去と売却損益の認識はファクタリング（売却）、顧客がファイナンス会社へ直接支払うのは通知方式、信用リスクの移転はノンリコース・ファクタリングの説明です。

questions.stem: Entity Q factors $400,000 of accounts receivable without recourse. The factor charges a 2.5% finance fee and withholds 6% of the receivables as a holdback for customer returns and allowances. What amount of cash does Entity Q receive at the date of transfer?
questions.explanation: Cash received = $400,000 − finance fee ($400,000 × 2.5% = $10,000) − holdback ($400,000 × 6% = $24,000) = $366,000. The holdback is recorded as a receivable from the factor (due from factor), not as a loss, because it is expected to be remitted after the return period. The amount of $390,000 deducts only the fee, $376,000 deducts only the holdback, and $342,000 results from deducting the holdback twice.
questions.explanationJa: 受取現金 = $400,000 − 手数料($400,000 × 2.5% = $10,000) − ホールドバック($400,000 × 6% = $24,000) = $366,000。ホールドバック（factor's holdback）は返品・値引き精算後に返還される見込みのため、損失ではなく「ファクター未収金（due from factor）」として資産計上します。$390,000は手数料のみ控除、$376,000はホールドバックのみ控除、$342,000はホールドバックを二重控除した誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Factoring With Recourse - Loss on Sale
questions.stem: Entity R factors $600,000 of accounts receivable with recourse, and the transfer qualifies as a sale under ASC 860. The factor charges a 3% finance fee and withholds a 4% holdback. The fair value of Entity R's recourse obligation is $10,000. What loss should Entity R recognize on the sale?
questions.explanation: When a with-recourse transfer qualifies as a sale, the loss equals the finance fee plus the fair value of the recourse obligation: ($600,000 × 3% = $18,000) + $10,000 = $28,000. The 4% holdback ($24,000) is a receivable from the factor and does not affect the loss because it is expected to be returned. The amount of $18,000 omits the recourse obligation, $42,000 incorrectly treats the holdback as part of the loss, and $10,000 is only the recourse obligation component.
questions.explanationJa: リコース付き譲渡が売却要件を満たす場合、売却損 = 手数料 ＋ 遡求義務（recourse obligation）の公正価値 = ($600,000 × 3% = $18,000) + $10,000 = $28,000 です。遡求義務は売却時に公正価値で負債計上され、その分損失が増加します。4%のホールドバック($24,000)は返還が見込まれるファクター未収金であり損失に含めません。$18,000は遡求義務を omit、$42,000はホールドバックを損失に含めた誤り、$10,000は遡求義務部分のみです。

questions.subtopic: ASC 860 - Effective Control Through Repurchase Agreement
questions.stem: Entity S transfers a portfolio of receivables to an unrelated financial institution for cash. The transfer agreement requires Entity S to repurchase the same receivables before their maturity at a fixed price. The receivables have been legally isolated from Entity S, and the transferee has the right to pledge them. How should Entity S account for the transfer?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a sale, because legal isolation and the transferee's right to pledge are both satisfied

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a secured borrowing, because the repurchase agreement means Entity S maintains effective control over the receivables

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a sale of the receivables with a separate forward contract recognized at fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a partial sale, derecognizing only the portion not subject to repurchase

questions.explanation: ASC 860 requires all three conditions for sale accounting: (1) legal isolation, (2) the transferee's right to pledge or exchange the assets, and (3) the transferor's surrender of effective control. An agreement that entitles and obligates the transferor to repurchase the transferred assets before maturity at a fixed price means the transferor maintains effective control, so the third condition fails and the entire transfer is accounted for as a secured borrowing — cash received is recorded as a liability and the receivables stay on the books. Meeting two of the three conditions is not sufficient for sale treatment, separating a forward contract does not cure the failed control condition, and partial derecognition is not available when effective control over the transferred assets is retained.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 860の売却処理には3要件すべての充足が必要です: ①法的隔離、②譲受人の担保差入・交換の権利、③譲渡人による実効的支配の放棄。満期前に固定価格で買い戻す権利と義務を譲渡人に与える契約は、譲渡人が実効的支配を保持していることを意味し、第3要件を満たさないため、譲渡全体を担保付借入として処理します（受取現金を負債計上し、債権は帳簿に残す）。3要件のうち2つの充足では売却処理は認められず、先渡契約の区分処理や部分的な認識中止で支配要件の不備を治癒することはできません。

questions.stem: Entity T sells a portfolio of receivables but retains the obligation to service them. Under ASC 860, in which situation should Entity T recognize a servicing liability rather than a servicing asset?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Whenever servicing is retained in any sale of receivables

choices.label: B
choices.text: When the servicing fee is exactly equal to adequate compensation

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the entity expects servicing costs to remain constant over the servicing period

choices.label: D
choices.text: When the benefits of servicing (fees and other revenues) are not expected to adequately compensate the entity for performing the servicing

questions.explanation: Under ASC 860-50, a servicer recognizes a servicing asset when the benefits of servicing (contractual fees, late charges, float) are expected to more than adequately compensate it for servicing, and a servicing liability when those benefits are expected to fall short of adequate compensation. Both are initially measured at fair value. Retaining servicing does not automatically create an asset or a liability; when the fee exactly equals adequate compensation, neither an asset nor a liability arises; and the constancy of servicing costs by itself does not determine which item is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 860-50では、サービシングの便益（契約手数料、延滞料、フロート等）が適正報酬（adequate compensation）を上回ると見込まれる場合はサービシング資産を、下回ると見込まれる場合はサービシング負債を認識します。いずれも当初は公正価値で測定します。サービシング業務を継続するだけでは自動的に資産・負債は生じず、手数料が適正報酬と一致する場合はどちらも認識しません。サービシングコストの一定性自体は資産・負債の判定基準ではありません。

questions.stem: Entity U sells products for $800,000 during the year and, based on historical experience, estimates that 6% of sales will be returned. The cost of the products is 60% of the selling price. Under ASC 606, what amount should Entity U recognize as a return asset (right to recover products from customers)?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606, expected returns create two separate items: a refund liability measured at the selling price of expected returns ($800,000 × 6% = $48,000) and a return asset measured at the carrying amount (cost) of the products expected to be recovered: $48,000 × 60% = $28,800. The amount of $48,000 is the refund liability, not the return asset; $19,200 incorrectly uses the 40% gross margin instead of the 60% cost ratio; and $0 would ignore the requirement to recognize the right to recover returned goods.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606では、予想返品について2つの項目を区分して認識します: 返金負債（refund liability）は予想返品の販売価格で測定（$800,000 × 6% = $48,000）、返品資産（return asset）は回収が見込まれる商品の帳簿価額（原価）で測定します: $48,000 × 60% = $28,800。$48,000は返金負債であって返品資産ではなく、$19,200は原価率60%ではなく粗利率40%を使った誤り、$0は返品商品の回収権の認識義務を無視しています。なお返品資産と返金負債は相殺表示できません。

questions.stem: At year-end, Entity V's accounts receivable subsidiary ledger shows customer accounts with debit balances totaling $520,000 and customer accounts with credit balances (arising from overpayments and advance payments) totaling $30,000. How should these amounts be presented on the balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Accounts receivable of $490,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Accounts receivable of $520,000 as an asset and $30,000 as a liability

choices.label: C
choices.text: Accounts receivable of $550,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Accounts receivable of $520,000 with the $30,000 disclosed only in the notes

questions.explanation: Customer accounts with credit balances represent obligations to customers (refunds or future deliveries) and must be reported as liabilities; they cannot be offset against other customers' debit balances because the right of setoff does not exist between different customers. Therefore receivables of $520,000 are reported as an asset and $30,000 as a liability. Reporting $490,000 improperly nets the two, $550,000 improperly adds the credit balances to the asset, and note disclosure alone fails to recognize the liability on the balance sheet.
questions.explanationJa: 貸方残高の顧客勘定は顧客に対する義務（返金または将来の引渡し）を表すため負債として表示しなければなりません。異なる顧客間には相殺権（right of setoff）が存在しないため、他の顧客の借方残高と相殺できません（ASC 210-20）。よって売掛金$520,000を資産、$30,000を負債として表示します。$490,000は不適切な純額表示、$550,000は貸方残高を資産に加算した誤り、注記開示のみでは負債の貸借対照表計上義務を満たしません。

questions.subtopic: Serial Note - Total Interest over Life
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Entity W accepts a $150,000 note receivable requiring three equal annual principal payments of $50,000 each December 31, plus interest at 8% on the outstanding principal balance. What total interest income will Entity W earn over the life of the note?
questions.explanation: Interest is computed each year on the declining outstanding principal: Year 1: $150,000 × 8% = $12,000; Year 2: $100,000 × 8% = $8,000; Year 3: $50,000 × 8% = $4,000. Total = $24,000. The amount of $36,000 incorrectly applies 8% to the full $150,000 for all three years, $12,000 is only the first year's interest, and $18,000 results from averaging incorrectly rather than computing interest on the actual outstanding balances.
questions.explanationJa: 利息は毎年、逓減する元本残高に対して計算します: Year 1: $150,000 × 8% = $12,000、Year 2: $100,000 × 8% = $8,000、Year 3: $50,000 × 8% = $4,000。合計$24,000です。$36,000は3年間とも全額$150,000に8%を適用した誤り、$12,000は初年度の利息のみ、$18,000は実際の元本残高ではなく不正確な平均で計算した誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Discounted Note Dishonored - Payment to Bank
questions.stem: Entity X discounted a customer's $100,000, 6%, 1-year note at a bank with recourse, accounted for as a secured borrowing. At maturity, the maker dishonors the note, and the bank charges Entity X the maturity value plus a $500 protest fee. Entity X expects to collect from the maker. What amount should Entity X record as a receivable from the maker?
questions.explanation: Because the note was discounted with recourse, Entity X must pay the bank when the maker dishonors the note: maturity value of $100,000 + ($100,000 × 6%) = $106,000, plus the $500 protest fee, for a total of $106,500. The entire amount paid, including the protest fee, becomes a legitimate claim against the maker and is recorded as a receivable. The amount of $100,000 omits both interest and the fee, $106,000 omits the protest fee, and $100,500 omits the interest component.
questions.explanationJa: リコース付きで割引した手形が不渡りになった場合、譲渡人は銀行に支払義務を負います: 満期価値 $100,000 + ($100,000 × 6%) = $106,000 に拒絶証書手数料（protest fee）$500を加えた$106,500です。支払総額（手数料含む）は振出人に対する正当な請求権となるため、全額を未収金として計上します。$100,000は利息と手数料を omit、$106,000は手数料を omit、$100,500は利息を omit した誤りです。

questions.stem: Under ASC 835-30, interest must generally be imputed on a note receivable when the stated rate is unreasonable. To which of the following receivables does the interest imputation requirement NOT apply?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A 3-year non-interest-bearing note received in exchange for equipment

choices.label: B
choices.text: A 5-year note from a customer bearing interest at 1% when the market rate is 9%

choices.label: C
choices.text: A 4-year note received in exchange for land when the note's face amount materially exceeds the land's fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: A trade receivable arising in the normal course of business due on customary terms within one year

questions.explanation: ASC 835-30 explicitly excludes receivables and payables arising from transactions with customers or suppliers in the normal course of business that are due on customary trade terms not exceeding approximately one year. Such short-term trade receivables are recorded at the invoice amount without discounting. A multi-year non-interest-bearing note, a note with a stated rate far below market, and a note whose face amount materially differs from the fair value of the property exchanged all require imputation of interest at the market rate.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-30は、通常の営業過程で顧客・仕入先との取引から生じ、概ね1年以内の通常の取引条件で決済される債権・債務を利息の帰属計算（imputation）の適用範囲から明示的に除外しています。このような短期営業債権は割引計算せず請求額で計上します。複数年の無利息手形、表面利率が市場利率を大幅に下回る手形、額面が交換対象資産の公正価値と著しく乖離する手形は、いずれも市場利率による利息の帰属計算が必要です。

questions.subtopic: Discounting With Recourse - Contingent Liability Disclosure
questions.stem: Entity Y discounts several customer notes receivable at a bank with recourse, and the transfers are accounted for as sales. Until the makers pay the notes at maturity, how should Entity Y report its recourse exposure beyond the recognized recourse obligation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Disclose the contingent liability for the notes discounted with recourse

choices.label: B
choices.text: Accrue the full maturity value of all discounted notes as a liability

choices.label: C
choices.text: No accrual or disclosure is needed once the notes are derecognized

choices.label: D
choices.text: Re-recognize the notes receivable until maturity

questions.explanation: When notes are discounted with recourse and treated as sales, the transferor remains contingently liable if the makers fail to pay. The fair value of the recourse obligation is recognized at the transfer date, and the remaining contingent exposure (the maturity value of notes that could be charged back) is disclosed as a contingent liability under ASC 450. Accruing the full maturity value would overstate liabilities because default by all makers is not probable, silence is inconsistent with the disclosure requirements for guarantees and contingencies, and re-recognizing sold notes contradicts derecognition under ASC 860.
questions.explanationJa: リコース付きで手形を割引し売却処理した場合でも、振出人が不払いとなれば譲渡人は支払義務を負うため偶発債務が残ります。譲渡日に遡求義務の公正価値を負債計上したうえで、残りの偶発的エクスポージャー（買戻し請求され得る満期価値）はASC 450に基づき偶発債務として注記開示します。全手形の満期価値の負債計上は、全振出人の不払いがprobableでない以上過大計上です。開示の省略は保証・偶発事象の開示要求に反し、売却済手形の再認識はASC 860の認識中止と矛盾します。

questions.subtopic: Percentage of Sales vs Aging - Conceptual Emphasis
questions.stem: Which of the following best describes the conceptual difference between the percentage-of-sales method and the aging-of-receivables method for estimating credit losses?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The percentage-of-sales method emphasizes the net realizable value of receivables, while aging emphasizes expense matching

choices.label: B
choices.text: The percentage-of-sales method emphasizes matching expense with revenue (income statement), while aging emphasizes the net realizable value of receivables (balance sheet)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both methods compute the required ending balance of the allowance and back into the expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: Both methods compute the expense directly and ignore the existing allowance balance

questions.explanation: The percentage-of-sales method applies a loss rate to credit sales and records that amount directly as expense, emphasizing the matching of credit loss expense with the revenue of the period — an income statement orientation. The aging method estimates the required ending allowance from the composition of outstanding receivables, emphasizing the proper valuation (net realizable value) of receivables — a balance sheet orientation; the expense is then the amount needed to adjust the allowance. The descriptions that reverse the two orientations, that treat both as balance-sheet approaches, or that treat both as direct expense computations each mischaracterize one of the methods.
questions.explanationJa: 売上高比率法は信用売上に損失率を乗じた額を直接費用計上するため、当期収益と貸倒費用の対応（matching）を重視する損益計算書アプローチです。エイジング法は売掛金の構成から必要な期末引当金残高を見積もるため、債権の適正評価（正味実現可能価額）を重視する貸借対照表アプローチであり、費用は引当金を必要残高に調整する差額として算定されます。両者の志向を逆にした記述、両方を残高アプローチとする記述、両方を直接費用計算とする記述はいずれも一方の方法を誤って特徴づけています。

questions.subtopic: Aging Method - Pre-Adjustment Debit Balance with Recoveries
questions.stem: Entity Z's allowance for credit losses had a beginning credit balance of $20,000. During the year, accounts of $26,000 were written off and $4,000 of previously written-off accounts were recovered and collected. At year-end, an aging analysis indicates a required allowance of $35,000. What is credit loss expense for the year?
questions.explanation: The pre-adjustment allowance balance is: $20,000 − $26,000 + $4,000 = ($2,000) debit balance. To reach the required credit balance of $35,000, the expense must cover both the deficit and the required balance: $35,000 + $2,000 = $37,000. The amount of $35,000 ignores the pre-adjustment balance, $33,000 incorrectly treats the $2,000 deficit as a credit balance and subtracts it, and $41,000 ignores the $4,000 of recoveries ($35,000 + $6,000).
questions.explanationJa: 調整前の引当金残高は $20,000 − $26,000（償却） + $4,000（回収戻入） = ($2,000) の借方残高です。必要な貸方残高$35,000に到達するには、不足分も含めて繰り入れる必要があります: $35,000 + $2,000 = $37,000。$35,000は調整前残高を無視、$33,000は$2,000の借方残高を貸方残高と誤認して減算、$41,000は回収戻入$4,000を無視した誤り($35,000 + $6,000)です。

questions.subtopic: Discount on Notes Receivable - Presentation and Amortization
questions.stem: Entity AB holds a long-term non-interest-bearing note receivable recorded at present value with a related discount on notes receivable. Which of the following correctly describes the presentation and subsequent accounting for the discount?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The discount is reported as a deferred charge (asset) and expensed at maturity

choices.label: B
choices.text: The discount is reported as a current liability until the note matures

choices.label: C
choices.text: The discount is a contra-asset deducted from the face amount of the note and is amortized to interest income over the note's life using the effective interest method

choices.label: D
choices.text: The discount is charged directly to retained earnings at inception

questions.explanation: Discount on notes receivable is a valuation (contra-asset) account presented as a direct deduction from the face amount of the note, so the note appears at its amortized carrying amount. The discount is amortized into interest income over the life of the note using the effective interest method, increasing the carrying amount toward face value at maturity. The discount is not an asset or deferred charge of its own, it is not a liability because it does not represent an obligation, and charging it to retained earnings would bypass the income statement in violation of the comprehensive income concept.
questions.explanationJa: 受取手形割引額（discount on notes receivable）は評価勘定（контра資産）であり、手形の額面から直接控除する形で表示します。これにより手形は償却原価で表示されます。割引額は実効金利法により手形の存続期間にわたって受取利息へ償却され、帳簿価額は満期に向けて額面まで増加します（ASC 835-30）。割引額自体は独立した資産・繰延費用ではなく、義務でもないため負債でもありません。利益剰余金への直接賦課は損益計算書を迂回するため認められません。

questions.stem: Entity AC, whose normal operating cycle is two months, sells equipment to a customer and receives a note receivable due in three years. How should the note be classified on Entity AC's classified balance sheet at the end of the current year?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a noncurrent asset, because it is due beyond one year and beyond the operating cycle

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a current asset, because all receivables from customers are current

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a current asset, because notes receivable are always classified by legal form

choices.label: D
choices.text: Offset against the related equipment account

questions.explanation: Assets are classified as current when they are expected to be realized within one year or the operating cycle, whichever is longer. With a two-month operating cycle, the one-year test governs, and a note due in three years is realized well beyond that period, so it is noncurrent (with any portion due within one year reclassified as current as maturity approaches). Customer origin does not automatically make a receivable current, classification depends on timing of realization rather than legal form, and offsetting against equipment is improper because the equipment was sold and no right of setoff exists.
questions.explanationJa: 資産は、1年または正常営業循環期間のいずれか長い期間内に実現が見込まれる場合に流動資産に分類されます。営業循環が2か月の場合は1年基準が適用され、3年後満期の手形はそれを大きく超えるため固定（非流動）資産です（満期接近に伴い1年以内到来部分は流動へ振替）。顧客からの債権という理由だけで流動になるわけではなく、分類は法的形式ではなく実現時期に基づきます。設備は売却済みで相殺権も存在しないため、設備勘定との相殺は不適切です。

questions.stem: Entity AD factors its receivables every month. The factor charges a fee of 1.5% of the face amount of receivables factored, and the receivables would otherwise be collected in approximately 30 days. Assuming a 360-day year, what is the approximate effective annual cost of factoring as a financing source?
questions.explanation: The 1.5% fee buys 30 days of accelerated cash. Annualizing: 1.5% × (360 / 30) = 1.5% × 12 = 18%. This is why factoring is typically a relatively expensive financing source compared with bank borrowing. The rate of 1.5% is only the per-period cost, 9% would result from incorrectly assuming a 60-day collection period (6 periods per year), and 36% would result from incorrectly assuming a 15-day collection period (24 periods per year).
questions.explanationJa: 1.5%の手数料は30日分の資金回収の前倒しの対価です。年率換算すると 1.5% × (360 ÷ 30) = 1.5% × 12 = 18% となります。これがファクタリングが銀行借入に比べて割高な資金調達手段とされる理由です。1.5%は1期間あたりのコストにすぎず、9%は回収期間を60日（年6回転）、36%は15日（年24回転）と誤って仮定した場合の数値です。

questions.stem: At year-end, Entity A has gross accounts receivable of $950,000, an allowance for credit losses of $38,000, and expects customers to take cash discounts of $12,000 on outstanding balances. At what amount should accounts receivable be reported at net realizable value?
questions.explanation: Net realizable value is the amount of cash the entity expects to collect. Both the allowance for credit losses and the estimated cash discounts expected to be taken reduce gross receivables: $950,000 − $38,000 − $12,000 = $900,000. The amount of $912,000 deducts only the credit loss allowance and ignores expected discounts; $938,000 deducts only the discounts; $950,000 is the gross balance with no reduction at all.
questions.explanationJa: 正味実現可能価額（NRV）は回収が見込まれる現金額である。貸倒引当金と顧客が利用すると見込まれる現金値引の両方を総売掛金から控除する: $950,000 − $38,000 − $12,000 = $900,000。$912,000は貸倒引当金のみを控除し見込値引を無視した金額、$938,000は値引のみを控除した金額、$950,000は何も控除しない総額である。

questions.stem: Entity B estimates credit loss expense at 0.8% of net credit sales. Total sales for the year were $5,000,000, of which $1,200,000 were cash sales. Sales returns on credit sales were $300,000. What amount should Entity B report as credit loss expense for the year?
questions.explanation: The percentage applies to net credit sales only. Credit sales = $5,000,000 − $1,200,000 = $3,800,000; net credit sales = $3,800,000 − $300,000 returns = $3,500,000. Expense = $3,500,000 × 0.8% = $28,000. The amount of $30,400 fails to deduct the returns; $37,600 deducts returns but incorrectly includes cash sales; $40,000 applies the rate to total sales.
questions.explanationJa: 比率は純掛売上高にのみ適用する。掛売上 = $5,000,000 − 現金売上$1,200,000 = $3,800,000。純掛売上 = $3,800,000 − 返品$300,000 = $3,500,000。費用 = $3,500,000 × 0.8% = $28,000。$30,400は返品を控除し忘れた金額、$37,600は返品は控除したが現金売上を誤って含めた金額、$40,000は総売上高に比率を掛けた金額である。

questions.stem: Entity C's allowance for credit losses had a beginning credit balance of $50,000 and an ending credit balance of $49,000. During the year, credit loss expense of $35,000 was recognized and accounts of $42,000 were written off. What amount of previously written-off accounts was recovered during the year?
questions.explanation: Allowance rollforward: Beginning $50,000 + expense $35,000 − write-offs $42,000 + recoveries = ending $49,000. Recoveries = $49,000 − $43,000 = $6,000. The amount of $41,000 omits the credit loss expense from the rollforward; $36,000 omits the write-offs; $13,000 results from reversing the signs of expense and write-offs.
questions.explanationJa: 引当金のロールフォワード: 期首$50,000 + 費用$35,000 − 償却$42,000 + 回収額 = 期末$49,000。回収額 = $49,000 − $43,000 = $6,000。$41,000は費用をロールフォワードから漏らした場合、$36,000は償却を漏らした場合、$13,000は費用と償却の符号を逆にした場合の金額である。

questions.stem: In Year 3, Entity D obtains new information indicating that its historical loss rate for estimating uncollectible receivables should increase from 2% to 3%. How should Entity D account for this change?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Restate prior-year financial statements to reflect the 3% rate

choices.label: B
choices.text: Apply the 3% rate prospectively in Year 3 and future periods as a change in accounting estimate

choices.label: C
choices.text: Report the cumulative effect of the change as an adjustment to beginning retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose the change but continue using the 2% rate until the next fiscal year

questions.explanation: A revision of the estimated uncollectible percentage based on new information is a change in accounting estimate under ASC 250, accounted for prospectively in the period of change and future periods. Restating prior years is the treatment for error corrections, not estimate changes; a cumulative-effect adjustment to retained earnings applies to certain changes in accounting principle; and continuing to use a rate known to be outdated would misstate the current-period allowance.
questions.explanationJa: 新たな情報に基づく貸倒見積率の改定はASC 250上の会計上の見積りの変更であり、変更期および将来の期間に将来法（prospective）で処理する。過年度の遡及修正は誤謬の訂正の処理であり見積りの変更には適用されない。利益剰余金期首残高への累積影響調整は一部の会計方針の変更に適用される処理である。また、古いと判明した率を使い続けることは当期の引当金を誤表示させる。

questions.subtopic: Direct Write-Off vs Allowance - Income Effect
questions.stem: In Year 1, Entity E had credit sales of $1,500,000. Under the allowance method, it would estimate credit loss expense at 2% of credit sales. Actual accounts written off as uncollectible during Year 1 were $18,000. Compared with the allowance method, Year 1 net income under the direct write-off method would be:

choices.label: A
choices.text: $12,000 higher

choices.label: B
choices.text: $12,000 lower

choices.label: C
choices.text: $30,000 lower

choices.label: D
choices.text: The same under both methods

questions.explanation: Under the allowance method, expense = $1,500,000 × 2% = $30,000. Under the direct write-off method, expense equals actual write-offs of $18,000. Direct write-off expense is $12,000 lower, so net income is $12,000 higher. Stating that income is $12,000 lower reverses the comparison; $30,000 lower treats the allowance-method expense as an additional charge; and the two methods produce the same income only when estimated expense happens to equal actual write-offs, which is not the case here.
questions.explanationJa: 引当金法では費用 = $1,500,000 × 2% = $30,000。直接償却法では費用は実際の償却額$18,000。直接償却法の費用が$12,000少ないため、純利益は$12,000多くなる。「$12,000少ない」は比較の向きが逆である。「$30,000少ない」は引当金法の費用を追加費用として扱った誤り。両方法の利益が一致するのは見積費用と実際償却額が偶然一致する場合のみで、本問では一致しない。

questions.stem: Entity F sells merchandise to a customer and accepts a $30,000 non-interest-bearing trade note due in 60 days, consistent with customary trade terms. At what amount should Entity F initially record the note receivable?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Present value discounted at the market rate of interest

choices.label: B
choices.text: Present value discounted at the prime rate

choices.label: C
choices.text: Face amount of $30,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Face amount less an imputed discount amortized over 60 days

questions.explanation: ASC 835-30 excludes from interest imputation receivables arising from transactions with customers in the normal course of business that are due within approximately one year under customary trade terms. A 60-day trade note is therefore recorded at its face amount of $30,000. Discounting at a market rate or the prime rate, or recording an imputed discount, applies only to notes outside this scope exception, such as long-term notes with unreasonably low stated rates.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-30は、通常の営業過程で顧客との取引から生じ、慣行的な取引条件によりおおむね1年以内に決済される債権を利息の付与（imputation）の対象から除外している。したがって60日の営業手形は額面$30,000で計上する。市場利子率やプライムレートで割り引く処理、または付与割引を計上する処理は、このスコープ除外に該当しない手形（不当に低い表面利率の長期手形など）にのみ適用される。

questions.subtopic: Interest-Bearing Note at Discount - Total Interest over Life
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Entity G accepts a 3-year, $200,000 note receivable with a 4% stated rate (interest of $8,000 paid each December 31; principal due at maturity). The market rate for similar notes is 8%. The present value factors at 8% for 3 periods are 0.79383 (single sum) and 2.57710 (ordinary annuity). What total interest income will Entity G recognize over the life of the note?
questions.explanation: Initial carrying amount = ($200,000 × 0.79383) + ($8,000 × 2.57710) = $158,766 + $20,617 = $179,383. Total cash to be received = $200,000 principal + ($8,000 × 3) = $224,000. Total interest income over the life = $224,000 − $179,383 = $44,617, which equals the stated coupons of $24,000 plus the discount of $20,617 amortized to income. The amount of $24,000 counts only the stated coupons; $20,617 counts only the discount amortization; $48,000 applies 8% to the face amount for three years instead of to the carrying amount.

questions.subtopic: Zero-Coupon Note - Total Interest over Life
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Entity H accepts a 2-year, non-interest-bearing note with a face amount of $121,000 in exchange for services. The market rate of interest is 10%. What total interest income will Entity H recognize over the 2-year life of the note?
questions.explanation: The note is recorded at present value: $121,000 ÷ (1.10)² = $100,000. Total interest income over the life equals the full discount: $121,000 − $100,000 = $21,000 (Year 1: $100,000 × 10% = $10,000; Year 2: $110,000 × 10% = $11,000). The amount of $10,000 is only Year 1 interest; $24,200 applies 10% to the face amount for two years; $12,100 applies 10% to the face amount for one year.

questions.stem: Entity J receives a long-term note in exchange for property in a transaction with no established exchange price for the property and an unreasonable stated interest rate on the note. Under ASC 835-30, at what amount should the note initially be recorded?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The fair value of the property or the market value of the note, whichever is more clearly determinable

choices.label: B
choices.text: The face amount of the note

choices.label: C
choices.text: The carrying amount of the property surrendered

choices.label: D
choices.text: The face amount plus accrued interest at the stated rate

questions.explanation: When there is no established exchange price and the stated rate is unreasonable, the note is recorded at the fair value of the property exchanged or the market value of the note, whichever is more clearly determinable; if neither is determinable, the note is recorded at present value using an imputed rate. Recording at face amount ignores the time value of money when the stated rate is unreasonable. The carrying amount of the property surrendered is not a measure of the consideration received. Adding accrued interest at an unreasonable stated rate compounds the misstatement.
questions.explanationJa: 成立した交換価格がなく表面利率が不合理な場合、手形は交換した財産の公正価値または手形の市場価値のうち、より明確に決定できる方で計上する。いずれも決定できない場合は付与利子率（imputed rate）による現在価値で計上する。額面での計上は表面利率が不合理な場合に貨幣の時間価値を無視することになる。引き渡した財産の帳簿価額は受け取った対価の測定値ではない。不合理な表面利率による未収利息を加算する処理は誤表示を拡大させる。

questions.subtopic: Note Discounting - Basis of Bank Discount
questions.stem: When an entity discounts an interest-bearing note receivable at a bank before maturity, the bank's discount charge is computed by applying the discount rate to:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The face amount of the note for the full original term

choices.label: B
choices.text: The maturity value of the note for the time remaining to maturity

choices.label: C
choices.text: The carrying amount of the note for the time the holder held it

choices.label: D
choices.text: The face amount of the note for the time remaining to maturity

questions.explanation: The bank earns its return on the amount it will collect at maturity — the maturity value (face plus interest to maturity) — over the period it must wait, which is the remaining term. Discount = maturity value × discount rate × remaining time. Using the face amount understates the base for an interest-bearing note, and using the full original term or the holding period misstates the time over which the bank's funds are outstanding.
questions.explanationJa: 銀行は満期に回収する金額、すなわち満期価値（額面＋満期までの利息）に対して、自らが待つ期間（残存期間）にわたりリターンを得る。割引料 = 満期価値 × 割引率 × 残存期間。利付手形について額面を基礎に使うと計算基礎が過小になり、当初の全期間や保有期間を使うと銀行の資金が拘束される期間を誤って測定することになる。

questions.stem: Entity K holds a $100,000, 9%, 6-month note receivable dated April 1. On August 1, Entity K discounts the note at a bank at a 6% discount rate in a transfer that qualifies as a sale. What gain or loss should Entity K recognize on the transfer?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of $1,045

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gain of $3,455

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gain of $1,500

choices.label: D
choices.text: Gain of $455

questions.explanation: Maturity value = $100,000 + ($100,000 × 9% × 6/12) = $104,500. The note has 2 months remaining, so the bank discount = $104,500 × 6% × 2/12 = $1,045, and proceeds = $104,500 − $1,045 = $103,455. The carrying amount at the transfer date is face plus 4 months of accrued interest: $100,000 + ($100,000 × 9% × 4/12) = $103,000. Gain = $103,455 − $103,000 = $455. Because the bank's discount rate is below the note's stated rate, the transfer produces a gain. The $1,045 figure is the bank discount itself, not the loss; $3,455 compares proceeds with the face amount and ignores accrued interest; $1,500 is the 3% rate differential applied to face for 6 months, which is not how the gain is measured.
questions.explanationJa: 満期価値 = $100,000 + ($100,000 × 9% × 6/12) = $104,500。残存期間は2か月なので割引料 = $104,500 × 6% × 2/12 = $1,045、手取額 = $104,500 − $1,045 = $103,455。譲渡日の帳簿価額は額面＋4か月分の未収利息 = $100,000 + ($100,000 × 9% × 4/12) = $103,000。利得 = $103,455 − $103,000 = $455。銀行の割引率が手形の表面利率を下回るため利得が生じる。$1,045は割引料そのものであり損失ではない。$3,455は手取額を額面と比較し未収利息を無視した金額。$1,500は利率差3%を額面に6か月分適用した金額で、利得の測定方法として誤りである。

questions.stem: Entity L assigns $500,000 of accounts receivable to a finance company and borrows $400,000 at 12% annual interest on the assigned accounts. During the first month, Entity L collects $150,000 of the assigned receivables and remits this amount to the finance company plus $4,000 of accrued interest. What is the balance of the note payable after the first remittance?
questions.explanation: Only the principal portion of the remittance reduces the loan: $400,000 − $150,000 = $250,000. The $4,000 interest payment is interest expense and does not reduce principal. The amount of $246,000 incorrectly applies the entire $154,000 remittance against principal; $254,000 incorrectly adds the interest to the loan balance; $396,000 incorrectly reduces the loan by only the $4,000 interest payment instead of the collections.
